articles.title: Measuring ≠ Learning
articles.summary: Product metrics are valuable only when they answer a meaningful question and lead to a clear decision. Instead of tracking generic or convenient numbers, teams should define specific measures tied to the user behavior and outcomes they want to change.

articles.title: 46 thoughts on the near future
articles.summary: Rapid AI progress could unlock unprecedented abundance while also concentrating power, disrupting society, and creating major security and governance risks.

articles.title: The most profitable skill of the 21st century (not AI)
articles.summary: Understanding human nature helps you capture attention, communicate value, inspire action, and succeed across changing industries and technologies.

articles.title: Why OpenAI is merging Codex and ChatGPT and the future of knowledge work (69 minute video)
articles.summary: As AI makes building dramatically faster and cheaper, product work is becoming less about implementation and more about choosing the right problems, evaluating possibilities, and maintaining coherence and quality. The strongest contributors will pair deep expertise with agency, adaptability, and taste, using AI to explore broadly while applying human judgment to deliver exceptional products.

articles.title: Career advice in the age of AI
articles.summary: As AI handles more defined tasks, career success will depend on choosing meaningful problems, building a strong reputation, and delivering exceptional results.

articles.title: This Is How To Overcome Burnout: 5 Secrets From Philosophy
articles.summary: Burnout often stems from the belief that we should always be working, improving, or maximizing our potential. Recovery requires questioning that pressure, setting boundaries, resting without justification, allowing mental stillness, and deliberately deciding when something is complete.

articles.title: Never mind the prompts, here's the thinking
articles.summary: While AI might not accelerate design projects, it boosts productivity by helping teams align prototypes, specs, and documentation. This frees up time for better decision-making - a task where human judgment, context, and taste remain essential.

articles.title: How To Thrive At Work When You Think Too Much
articles.summary: Deep thinkers thrive by separating self-worth from work, adapting with intention, and protecting the curiosity that makes their work meaningful.

articles.title: The Emotional Cost of Finding Product-Market Fit
articles.summary: Finding product-market fit is emotionally difficult, but focused experiments, flexibility, reflection, and a trusted co-founder can make the uncertainty easier to manage.

articles.title: “Tell a story” is not helpful advice
articles.summary: Effective storytelling pairs a clear challenge with a satisfying resolution, using a structure suited to the audience's attention span.
